title: Medullary Index Calculator
description: Calculate the medullary index (MI = medulla diameter ÷ shaft diameter) of a hair cross-section and interpret it as human, ambiguous, or non-human. Drag two sliders to set the medulla and shaft diameters; the cross-section diagram and the MI value update live, with a color-coded result badge and a species comparison row (human, dog, cat, rodent).
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Hair Analysis
Trace Evidence
Medullary Index
Microscopy
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — calculate the medullary index and apply the interpretation rule to classify a hair.
chapter_title: Hair, Fiber, and Trace Evidence Analysis

educational.topic: Calculating and interpreting the medullary index of a hair
learningObjectives: Calculate the medullary index as the ratio of medulla diameter to overall shaft diameter.
Apply the interpretation rule: MI ≤ 0.33 is consistent with human hair, while MI ≥ 0.50 indicates likely non-human (animal) hair.
Compare a measured medullary index against typical species values to support a human vs. non-human classification.
educational.prerequisites: Understanding that a hair shaft has an outer cortex and an inner medulla; basic ratio arithmetic

pedagogical.interactionPattern: The student drags two sliders to set the medulla diameter and the overall shaft diameter (in µm). A hair cross-section diagram redraws live, showing the medulla as a dark inner circle inside the lighter cortex, with dimension arrows for both diameters. A readout panel shows the MI formula, the computed value to two decimals, and a color-coded result badge (green Human, orange Ambiguous, red Non-human likely). A 'New Sample' button loads preset diameter pairs.
pedagogical.assessment: Formative exploration: by sweeping the sliders the student discovers the human/ambiguous/non-human thresholds and reads how each ratio maps to a classification. A species comparison row (human, dog, cat, rodent) lets the student check a calculated value against typical reference indices.
pedagogical.modelType: Deterministic ratio model: MI = medulla ÷ shaft, drawn to scale on a SCALE_MAX of 120 µm; the interpretation thresholds (0.33 and 0.50) drive the badge color and explanatory note.
controls: New Sample button (loads preset diameter pairs)
